title: senecio zeylanicus
intro: Senecio zeylanicus, also known as Jala bindi or Wild marigold, is a flowering plant species native to the Indian subcontinent and Sri Lanka, belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and skin conditions, due to its medicinal properties of possessing alkaloids like jala bindin and other sesquiterpenes. The health benefits of Senecio zeylanicus include its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which help in alleviating symptoms of fever, reducing inflammation, and preventing infections. Senecio zeylanicus can be prepared as an herbal tea or used in topical applications, such as ointments and creams, to harness its medicinal benefits.
